## Sentinel is now your polkit authentication agent

This release ships **`sentinel-polkit-agent`** — a session-scoped polkit
authentication agent that replaces cosmic-osd / polkit-gnome /
polkit-kde for the duration of your session. polkit now consults
exactly **one** UI for `pkexec` and other graphical privilege
escalation, instead of two parallel dialogs racing each other.

## What's new

- **`crates/sentinel-polkit-agent`** — long-running per-session D-Bus
  service that implements `org.freedesktop.PolicyKit1.AuthenticationAgent`,
  delegates the user prompt to `sentinel-helper`, and satisfies polkit's
  cookie validation by invoking the system `polkit-agent-helper-1`.
- **`crates/sentinel-token`** — HMAC-SHA256 bypass-token issuer/verifier
  shared between the agent and `pam_sentinel`. Breaks the otherwise
  infinite recursion (agent → helper-1 → pam_sentinel → spawn dialog →
  forever). Constant-time verification, fail-open on mismatch.
- **`pam_sentinel.so` env-var bypass** — when invoked under
  `polkit-agent-helper-1` from our agent, reads `SENTINEL_AGENT_AUTH`,
  `SENTINEL_AGENT_COOKIE`, `SENTINEL_AGENT_ACTION_ID`, validates the
  HMAC against `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/sentinel-agent.secret`, returns
  `PAM_SUCCESS` directly so the dialog doesn't re-spawn.
- **XDG autostart deployment** at
  `/etc/xdg/autostart/sentinel-polkit-agent.desktop`. The compositor
  forks the agent as a direct child at session start so it inherits the
  graphical session's kernel sessionid (which is what polkit's
  `RegisterAuthenticationAgent` check requires). Filter:
  `OnlyShowIn=COSMIC;Hyprland;sway;Wayfire;wlroots;` and
  `NotShowIn=GNOME;KDE;XFCE;LXDE;Cinnamon;MATE;LXQt;`.
- **Transactional install migration** — `install.sh` records `VERSION`
  and `ENABLED` actions in the state file, prompts on 0.2.x → 0.3.0
  upgrade, rolls back cleanly on failure. New flags
  `--enable-polkit-agent` / `--no-polkit-agent`.

## Why XDG autostart, not `systemctl --user`

polkit's `RegisterAuthenticationAgent` checks that our process's
session matches the subject we claim to register for. It resolves the
session via the kernel `sessionid` (set by `audit_setloginuid` at
login, inherited through forks). systemd-user-managed processes
inherit `user@1000.service`'s sessionid (the *manager* session), which
differs from the user's *graphical* session. Polkit refuses with:

> Passed session and the session the caller is in differs.

Compositors fork XDG autostart entries as direct children, so they
inherit the graphical sessionid and polkit accepts. We ship the
systemd `--user` unit at `/usr/lib/systemd/user/sentinel-polkit-agent.service`
for completeness but don't enable it by default. See
[Polkit Agent wiki page](https://github.com/atayozcan/sentinel/wiki/Polkit-Agent).

## Upgrade

```bash
# AUR
yay -Syu sentinel              # or sentinel-git

# Debian / Ubuntu
sudo apt install ./sentinel_0.3.0-1_amd64.deb

# Fedora / openSUSE
sudo dnf install ./sentinel-0.3.0-1.x86_64.rpm

# From source
git pull && pkexec ./install.sh
```

**You must log out and log back in** for the polkit agent to activate
— XDG autostart entries are only processed at session start.

## Migration notes

- The install state file format gained `VERSION` and `ENABLED` actions.
  Old `uninstall.sh` (0.2.x) safely ignores unknown actions, so
  downgrade-uninstall remains lossless.
- `install.sh` detects 0.2.x state and prompts before enabling the
  polkit agent. Pass `--no-polkit-agent` to skip.
- The polkit agent ships as XDG autostart filtered by `OnlyShowIn` to
  compositors without their own agent. On GNOME / KDE / XFCE /
  Cinnamon / MATE / LXDE / LXQt the entry is suppressed and the
  desktop's existing agent stays in charge — `pam_sentinel.so` still
  gates `sudo` (if wired) and the polkit-1 PAM stack on those systems.

## Known issues

- **systemd-homed** users may need a second login if `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`
  isn't ready on first compositor startup. `journalctl --user -t
  sentinel-polkit-agent` shows the cause.
- If you previously had a different polkit agent installed (cosmic-osd,
  polkit-gnome, polkit-kde), check `/etc/xdg/autostart/` for
  competing entries and mask them so Sentinel registers first.
